title: A few questions regarding the Angels
display_title: A few questions regarding the Angels
wikitext:
[]: A few questions regarding the Angels...             Quote Post by rhfxz1s » Sat Jan 01, 2011 1:08 am Hey everyone, I just finished watching Evangelion for the first time and was really impressed. After stumbling across this site and reading the wiki for awhile, (which is extremely well put together, by the way) I've had most of my questions about the show answered. However, I'm still confused about a few things and I couldn't seem to find them in the wiki or anything. I apologize if i missed something obvious... That being said, I know the Angels are trying to reunite with Adam so they can destroy all Lilith based life and claim the planet as their own. But why does this happen 15 years after second impact? Are the Angels working together, or are they each representing different Adam-based species that want to control Earth? Why are there 17 of them? I understand that Seele learned about that from the Sea Scrolls, but is there any greater point or significance to sending that specific number of Angels? Maybe I'm just thinking too hard but I feel like these are questions that should have been answered. Thanks in advance!

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Sat Jan 01, 2011 2:04 am 1) It took fifteen years because that's apparently the Angel gestation period. Some argue that they just took this long in the same way that it takes a human being about nine months to develop; some claim that specific events triggered this. We don't know the answer here. 2) The Angels... the best answer is NEITHER. They're all the same sort of life (that is, they can coexist), but they're not really working together. Each is a newborn child. Their goals are similar, and if one wins, ALL win (since Armisael seems to have the ability to make more Angels), but that's all we know. 3) There's seventeen because Adam and Lilith were required to make Angels and humans respectively (they're not like the other Angels) and the others... we don't know why there were so many. There could have been ten hundred, there could have been two. Presumably, there were supposed to be more being born later or created, but Adam went critical before its work was done. (From an out of story perspective, there were fifteen because that's how many they had time to cover. Originally, there were to be 28, but things changed as the story was told) So, that's that.

[]:             Quote Post by rhfxz1s » Sun Jan 02, 2011 12:10 am Alright, thanks!

umably, there were supposed to be more being born later or created, but Adam went critical before its work was done.
